question: What are the current interest rates for personal loans?
answer: Personal loan interest rates currently range from 10.5% to 18% p.a. depending on your credit score and relationship with the bank. SBI offers from 10.5%, HDFC from 10.75%, ICICI from 11%, and Axis from 11.25%.

question: What are home loan interest rates in SBI?
answer: SBI home loan rates start from 8.5% p.a. for salaried individuals. Women borrowers get 0.05% concession. Rates are linked to credit score: 800+ (8.5%), 750-799 (8.75%), 700-749 (9%), 650-699 (9.5%).

question: What are HDFC Bank car loan rates?
answer: HDFC Bank car loan rates: New cars (9.25% - 12%), Used cars (12% - 16%). Electric vehicles get 0.5% concession. Special rates for HDFC salary account customers.

question: What are ICICI Bank education loan interest rates?
answer: ICICI Bank education loan rates: Studies in India (11% - 13%), Studies abroad (12% - 15%). Loan amount above ₹50 lakhs may have lower rates. Merit scholarships available for 90%+ scores.

question: What are Axis Bank personal loan rates?
answer: Axis Bank personal loan rates start from 11.25% p.a. for existing customers. New customers: 12% - 18%. Pre-approved offers available on Axis mobile app.

question: What are PNB home loan interest rates?
answer: PNB home loan rates: Up to ₹30 lakhs (8.5% - 9%), ₹30-75 lakhs (8.75% - 9.25%), Above ₹75 lakhs (9% - 9.5%). PNB customers get 0.25% concession.

question: What are Bank of Baroda car loan rates?
answer: Bank of Baroda car loan rates: New cars (9.5% - 13%), Used cars (12% - 15%). Electric vehicles: 8.75% - 11%. Special rates for women: 0.10% concession.

question: What are Canara Bank education loan rates?
answer: Canara Bank education loan: Up to ₹4 lakhs (10.5% - 12%), ₹4-10 lakhs (11% - 13%), Above ₹10 lakhs (11.5% - 14%). No collateral for loans up to ₹7.5 lakhs.

question: What are Kotak Mahindra Bank personal loan rates?
answer: Kotak Mahindra personal loan rates: 11% - 17% p.a. Instant approval for Kotak account holders. 811 account holders get special rates.

question: What are Union Bank home loan rates?
answer: Union Bank home loan rates: Up to ₹30 lakhs (8.6% - 9.1%), ₹30-75 lakhs (8.85% - 9.35%), Above ₹75 lakhs (9.1% - 9.6%). No processing fee for women.

question: What are IDFC First Bank car loan rates?
answer: IDFC First Bank car loan: New cars (9.75% - 13%), Used cars (13% - 16%). Green vehicles: 9.25% - 12%. Zero processing fee for first-time buyers.

question: What are Yes Bank education loan rates?
answer: Yes Bank education loan: India (11.5% - 14%), Abroad (12.5% - 16%). Merit scholarship: 1% concession for 90%+ in XII. Loan amount up to ₹1.5 crore for abroad studies.

question: What are IndusInd Bank personal loan rates?
answer: IndusInd Bank personal loan: 11.5% - 18% p.a. Special rates for professionals: Doctors, CAs, Architects get 0.5% concession. Pre-approved offers on IndusInd app.

question: What are Bank of India home loan rates?
answer: Bank of India home loan: Up to ₹30 lakhs (8.55% - 9.05%), ₹30-75 lakhs (8.8% - 9.3%), Above ₹75 lakhs (9.05% - 9.55%). Star customers get 0.10% concession.

question: What are Central Bank car loan rates?
answer: Central Bank car loan: New cars (9.75% - 13%), Used cars (13% - 16%). Electric vehicles: 9.25% - 12%. Government employees get 0.25% concession.

question: What are Indian Bank education loan rates?
answer: Indian Bank education loan: Up to ₹4 lakhs (10.25% - 11.75%), ₹4-10 lakhs (10.75% - 12.75%), Above ₹10 lakhs (11.25% - 13.75%). Girl students get 0.50% concession.

question: What are UCO Bank personal loan rates?
answer: UCO Bank personal loan: 11% - 16% p.a. Pensioners get special rates: 10.5% - 14%. UCO account holders get faster approval.

question: What are Syndicate Bank home loan rates?
answer: Syndicate Bank home loan: Up to ₹30 lakhs (8.6% - 9.1%), ₹30-75 lakhs (8.85% - 9.35%), Above ₹75 lakhs (9.1% - 9.6%). Low income group schemes available.

question: What are Corporation Bank car loan rates?
answer: Corporation Bank car loan: New cars (9.5% - 12.5%), Used cars (12.5% - 15.5%). Women: 0.10% concession. Green vehicles: Additional 0.25% off.

question: What are Andhra Bank education loan rates?
answer: Andhra Bank education loan: India (11% - 13.5%), Abroad (12% - 15%). Merit scholarship: 1% off for 95%+ in XII. Loan up to ₹20 lakhs without collateral.

question: Am I eligible for SBI personal loan?
answer: SBI personal loan eligibility: Age 21-60 years, Monthly income ₹25,000+, Credit score 650+, Employment 1+ years. SBI salary account holders get faster approval.

question: Am I eligible for HDFC home loan?
answer: HDFC home loan eligibility: Age 21-65 years, Income ₹30,000+/month, Credit score 650+, Property value min ₹10 lakhs. Joint applicants increase eligibility by 40%.

question: Am I eligible for ICICI car loan?
answer: ICICI car loan eligibility: Age 21-60 years, Income ₹20,000+/month, Credit score 600+. New cars: Min income ₹15,000. Used cars: Min income ₹20,000, max car age 5 years.

question: Am I eligible for Axis education loan?
answer: Axis education loan eligibility: Age 16-35 years, Admission to recognized course, Co-applicant with income required. For loans >₹4 lakhs: Co-applicant income ₹25,000+/month.

question: Am I eligible for PNB personal loan?
answer: PNB personal loan eligibility: Age 21-60 years, Income ₹20,000+/month, Credit score 650+, PNB account holders get priority. Pensioners: Age up to 70 years, pension ₹10,000+.

question: Am I eligible for Canara Bank car loan?
answer: Canara Bank car loan: Age 21-65 years, Income ₹15,000+/month, Credit score 600+. For used cars: Car age <5 years, income ₹20,000+. Government employees: Simplified docs.

question: Am I eligible for Union Bank personal loan?
answer: Union Bank personal loan: Age 21-60 years, Income ₹20,000+/month, Credit score 650+. Government employees: Income ₹15,000+, simplified process. Pensioners: Age up to 75 years.

question: Am I eligible for IDFC home loan?
answer: IDFC home loan: Age 21-65 years, Income ₹30,000+/month, Credit score 700+. Self-employed: 3 years ITR, business vintage 3+ years. Salaried: 2 years experience.

question: Am I eligible for Yes Bank car loan?

question: Am I eligible for Central Bank home loan?
answer: Central Bank home loan: Age 21-70 years, Income ₹25,000+/month, Credit score 650+. Rural areas: Lower income criteria (₹15,000+). Agricultural land loan also available.

question: Am I eligible for Corporation home loan?

question: Am I eligible for Andhra car loan?

question: Am I eligible for Punjab & Sind education loan?
answer: Punjab & Sind education loan: Student age 16-40, Co-applicant income ₹20,000+/month. For minority communities: Special schemes, lower interest rates.

question: What documents needed for SBI personal loan?
answer: SBI personal loan documents: Identity (Aadhar/PAN), Address proof, Last 3 months salary slips, Form 16, Last 6 months bank statements, 2 passport photos.

question: What documents needed for ICICI car loan?

question: How to improve credit score for SBI loan?
answer: Improve credit score for SBI: Pay all EMIs on time, Keep credit utilization <30%, Don't close old cards, Check CIBIL report yearly, Dispute errors, Have mix of loans.

question: What is minimum credit score for HDFC home loan?
answer: HDFC home loan minimum credit score: 650 for approval, 750+ for best rates. Below 650: Higher interest,可能需要 additional security.

question: How to check CIBIL score for free?
answer: Free CIBIL score check: CRED app, Paytm, Google Pay, Amazon Pay, Bank apps (SBI YONO, HDFC NetBanking), Official CIBIL site (1 free/year), Experian, Equifax.

question: What is good credit score for Axis car loan?
answer: Axis car loan credit score: 750+ (Best rates), 700-749 (Good rates), 650-699 (Moderate rates), Below 650 (Higher rates,可能 approval issues).

question: How long does credit score take to improve?
answer: Credit score improvement timeline: On-time payments (3-6 months), Debt reduction (2-3 months), Error correction (1-2 months), Full recovery from default (12-24 months).

question: What is CIBIL score range?
answer: CIBIL score range: 300-900. Excellent (800-900), Good (750-799), Fair (700-749), Poor (650-699), Bad (600-649), Very Bad (300-599).

question: Does checking credit score affect it?
answer: Soft inquiries (self-check, bank pre-approved offers) do NOT affect score. Hard inquiries (loan applications) may affect slightly (5-10 points). Multiple inquiries in short time harmful.

question: How to get free credit report from CIBIL?
answer: Get free CIBIL report: Visit cibil.com, Click 'Get your free credit report', Register with PAN, Verify identity, Download report. One free report per year.

question: What is EMI and how is it calculated?
answer: EMI = [P x R x (1+R)^N] / [(1+R)^N-1]. P=Principal, R=Monthly interest rate, N=Tenure months. Example: ₹10 lakhs at 10.5% for 5 years = ₹21,498/month.

question: How to calculate EMI for home loan?
answer: Home loan EMI example: ₹50 lakhs at 8.5% for 20 years = ₹43,391/month. Use EMI calculator. Factors: Amount, Rate, Tenure. Longer tenure = Lower EMI but more interest.

question: Can I prepay my loan without charges?
answer: Floating rate loans: No prepayment charges. Fixed rate loans: 2-5% charges. Partial prepayment: Min ₹10,000, allowed after 6-12 months. Some banks offer 1 free prepayment/year.

question: What happens if I miss EMI payment?
answer: Missed EMI consequences: Late fee (2% per month), Credit score drops 50-100 points, Collection calls, NPA after 90 days, Legal action possible, Asset seizure in secured loans.

question: What are processing fees for SBI loans?
answer: SBI processing fees: Personal loan (1-1.5% + GST), Home loan (0.35-0.5% + GST), Car loan (0.5-1% + GST), Education loan (0.5-1% + GST). Waived for women/COVID warriors.

question: What are processing fees for HDFC loans?
answer: HDFC processing fees: Personal loan (1-2.5% + GST), Home loan (0.5-1% + GST), Car loan (1-2% + GST), Education loan (1-1.5% + GST). Pre-approved offers have zero fees.

question: How long does SBI personal loan approval take?
answer: SBI personal loan approval: Existing customers (2-4 hours), New customers (24-48 hours), Pre-approved offers (10 minutes). Disbursement within 24 hours of approval.

question: Can I take multiple loans from different banks?
answer: Yes, you can have multiple loans. Considerations: FOIR <50%, Each application affects credit score, Total EMI should not exceed 50% of income, Maintain repayment history.

question: What is loan tenure for SBI home loan?
answer: SBI home loan tenure: Maximum 30 years, Up to retirement age (salaried), Up to 70 years (self-employed). Longer tenure available for younger applicants.

question: What is EMI for ₹10 lakhs personal loan?
answer: EMI for ₹10 lakhs personal loan: 3 years (₹32,500 at 12%), 4 years (₹26,800 at 12%), 5 years (₹22,800 at 12%). Use EMI calculator for exact amount.

question: Can I get loan if I'm self-employed without ITR?
answer: Self-employed without ITR: Difficult to get loans. Options: Business proof, GST returns (1 year), Bank statements (12 months), Alternate income proof. Higher interest rates.

question: What is overdraft facility against FD?
answer: Overdraft against FD: Up to 90% of FD amount, Interest only on used amount, Repayment flexible, Interest rate FD rate + 1-2%, Quick approval, No income proof needed.

question: What is loan against property (LAP)?
answer: Loan Against Property: Up to 60-70% of property value, Interest rates 9-12%, Tenure up to 15 years, For business/personal needs, Lower rates than personal loans.

question: What is gold loan interest rate in SBI?
answer: SBI gold loan rates: 7.5% - 9% p.a., Loan up to 75% of gold value, No income proof needed, Quick disbursement, Repayment flexible, Interest only on used amount.

question: What is two-wheeler loan interest rate?
answer: Two-wheeler loan rates: 10% - 15% p.a., Loan up to 90% of bike value, Tenure 12-48 months, Down payment 10-20%, Quick approval, Minimal documents.

question: What is business loan interest rate?
answer: Business loan rates: MUDRA loans (8-11%), MSME loans (10-14%), Term loans (11-16%), Working capital (12-15%). Rates depend on turnover, vintage, credit score.

question: What is credit card loan interest rate?
answer: Credit card loan/EMI rates: 12% - 24% p.a., Convert purchases to EMI, No additional documents, Instant approval, Available on app, Lower than revolving credit.

question: What is doctor loan scheme in SBI?
answer: SBI doctor loan: Up to ₹50 lakhs, 9.5% - 11% interest, For clinic setup/equipment, 7 years tenure, No collateral up to ₹20 lakhs, Special for medical professionals.

question: What is CA loan scheme in HDFC?
answer: HDFC CA loan: Up to ₹75 lakhs, 9.5% - 11.5% interest, For practice setup/equipment, 8 years tenure, Flexible repayment, Special for chartered accountants.

question: What is pensioner loan scheme?
answer: Pensioner loan: Age up to 75-80 years, Loan up to ₹10 lakhs, Interest 10-13%, Pension as income proof, No employment proof needed, Quick approval.

question: What is farmer loan scheme in SBI?
answer: SBI farmer loan: Kisan Credit Card (7-9%), Crop loan (7-8.5%), Farm equipment loan (9-11%), Interest subvention for prompt payers, Government subsidy available.

question: What is women loan scheme in banks?
answer: Women loan schemes: 0.05-0.25% interest concession, Lower processing fees, Special schemes (Bharat Mahila Bank, Stree Shakti), Priority processing, Government schemes available.

question: What is NRI loan eligibility in SBI?
answer: SBI NRI loan: Age 24-65 years, Income $2,000+/month, NRE/NRO account required, Property in India, Co-applicant allowed, Higher interest for NRI.

question: What is loan against shares/securities?
answer: Loan against shares: Up to 50% of share value, Interest 9-12%, For listed shares, Quick disbursement, No income proof, Shares held in demat, Margin call if value drops.

question: What is loan against insurance policy?
answer: Loan against insurance: Up to 90% of surrender value, Interest 10-12%, For traditional plans, ULIPs, Quick processing, Policy continues, No repayment fixed schedule.

question: What is solar loan scheme?
answer: Solar loan: For rooftop/solar plants, Interest 7-9% with subsidy, Tenure up to 10 years, Government subsidy available, Green energy focus, Lower rates.

question: What is electric vehicle (EV) loan?
answer: EV loan: 0.25-0.5% concession on car loans, Special schemes from banks, Faster approval, Government subsidy pass-through, Lower EMI, Green initiative.

question: What is home renovation loan?
answer: Home renovation loan: Up to ₹20 lakhs, Interest 9-12%, Tenure up to 7 years, For repair/renovation, No collateral, Quick approval, Home improvement focus.

question: What is plot loan?
answer: Plot loan: For land purchase, Interest 8.5-11%, Tenure up to 15 years, Loan up to 70-80% of plot value, Construction later, Higher rates than home loan.

question: What is balance transfer facility?
answer: Balance transfer: Move loan to another bank for lower interest, Processing fee 0.5-1%, Save on interest, Top-up available, Better terms, Improved credit score.

question: What is loan moratorium period?
answer: Moratorium period: Loan repayment holiday, Interest accrues during period, Available for education loans (course + 1 year), Some banks offer for hardship cases, Extends tenure.

question: What is CIBIL default impact?
answer: CIBIL default impact: Score drops 100-200 points, Loan rejection for 2-3 years, Higher interest future loans, Negative remark for 7 years, Difficulty in new credit.

question: What is the difference between fixed and floating interest rate?
answer: Fixed rate: Constant EMI, Protection from rate hikes, Usually higher initial rate. Floating rate: Changes with market, Lower initial rate, Benefits from rate cuts, Preferred for long tenure.

question: What is the current repo rate and its impact on loans?
answer: Current repo rate: 6.5% (RBI). Impact: Repo rate hike increases loan EMIs, Repo rate cut decreases EMIs, External benchmark linked loans change immediately, Fixed rates unaffected.

question: What is MCLR and how does it affect loans?
answer: MCLR (Marginal Cost of Funds based Lending Rate): Internal benchmark for loans, Reset every year, Affects floating rate loans, Lower MCLR means lower EMI, Bank specific.

question: What is the maximum loan amount for SBI personal loan?
answer: SBI personal loan maximum: ₹25 lakhs for salaried, ₹15 lakhs for pensioners, ₹30 lakhs for government employees, Based on income multiple (15-20 times monthly income).

question: What is the maximum home loan amount from SBI?
answer: SBI home loan maximum: Up to ₹5 crore for salaried, Up to ₹10 crore for high net worth, Based on property value (80-90% of property cost), FOIR based calculation.
